learning_outcomes.description: Explain auditor's statutory responsibilities and the nature and functions of auditing in the context of the audit engagement/different types of audits.
Analyse the ethical and legal issues faced by auditors to form professional judgments on the appropriate course of action.

Apply the stages of audit planning and preliminary procedures to form an understanding of the audit client and its environment in order to develop audit strategies and design appropriate audit procedures.

Assess the audit client's risks, including carrying out an evaluation of the client's internal control system, and design appropriate audit procedures to identify any material misstatement in the financial statement.
Design audit procedures to complete the audit (including testing for post balance day events) and use professional judgment to form an audit opinion.
Work on audit issues as a member of a team.

assessments.task_description:
The in-semester exam will be scheduled by UQ Central for a Saturday Exam.

  • The exam time/date will be announced by the UQ Central Examination Office later in the semester. Please keep an eye on your personalised UQ web sites.
  • The exam will be based on the first four topics, including lectures and tutorials.
  • The exam contains multiple choice questions and written questions.
  • The written questions include practical/problem-solving questions that require you to analyse mini cases/scenarios and apply relevant audit theories/regulations/techniques to form appropriate judgments/decisions.
  • The written questions usually require you to demonstrate your understanding about important aspects of the audit process.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.assessment_title: Group Assignment - Case Study: Audit Planning and Testing
assessments.due_date:
16/10/2026 4:00 pm

Upload your case study on Blackboard via Turn-it-in before the due date. Only one student needs to submit the case study for the group but please clearly specify the identification details of all team members on the first page of the case study file.
assessments.task_description:
This is a group case study designed to give you some practical experience in audit planning, the application of the audit risk model, tests of control procedures, and substantive testing. Please make sure you are up to date with all course readings and class materials before undertaking the case study. 

Students need to form a group to work on the case study together. A penalty will apply if students submit this assignment as an individual assignment without the course coordinator's approval. More information will be available on Blackboard later to help you find team members. Lectures and tutorial classes are a good place to find potential team members.

The case study contains a number of questions. Further instructions will be available on the Course Blackboard site.

Important Submission Details:

  • Include names of each group member in your submission.
  • Save your case study as a Word document. PDF submissions will not be accepted.
  • All group members need to complete a peer review section on Blackboard, the results of the peer evaluation may affect your individual mark for this assessment.
  • Please avoid submitting your case study at the last minute because the system may be down or may malfunction due to the number of submission attempts at that time. Late submission without extension approval will be penalised according to the university policy.
  • After submission, take a screenshot of your receipt as proof. Retain this receipt for your records.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The assignment must be submitted electronically on Blackboard through Turnitin.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This exam is a closed book, invigilated exam .

The exam time/date will be announced by the UQ Central Examination Office later in the semester.

The final exam contains about 4 to 5 written questions. Each question contains a number of short answer /short essay /problem solving /application questions.

The written questions focus on problem-solving, which involves analysing cases/scenarios and applying relevant audit theories/ regulations/ techniques to form appropriate judgments/ decisions.

Exam questions also require you to demonstrate your understanding of important aspects of the audit process.

More details of the final exam will be made available towards the end of the semester.

You are advised that ALL areas of the course are examinable and thus leaving out any section of the course material from your study plans would be ill-advised.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Topic 1: Introduction and Audit Objectives

In Week 1, we will discuss what an audit is about, different types of audits and auditors, financial statement cycles, and various audit objectives. Relevant readings: Arens (Chapters 1 and 4).
learning_activities.topic_description: Topic 1: Introduction and Audit Objectives In Week 1, we will discuss what an audit is about, different types of audits and auditors, financial statement cycles, and various audit objectives. Relevant readings: Arens (Chapters 1 and 4).

learning_activities.topic: Tutorial 1: Introduction and Audit Objectives
learning_activities.topic_description: Tutorial 1: Introduction and Audit Objectives

learning_activities.topic:
Topic 2: Audit Risk Model and Audit Evidence

In Topic 2, we will introduce various ways auditors collect audit evidence and briefly discuss the components of the audit risk model for planning. Relevant reading: Arens (Chapters 5 and 7).
learning_activities.topic_title: Topic 2: Audit Risk Model and Audit Evidence
learning_activities.topic_description: Topic 2: Audit Risk Model and Audit Evidence In Topic 2, we will introduce various ways auditors collect audit evidence and briefly discuss the components of the audit risk model for planning. Relevant reading: Arens (Chapters 5 and 7).

EKKA Public Holiday – Wednesday, 12 August 2026 – Our Wednesday tutorials will be affected by the public holiday. Please attend alternative tutorials for this week only.

learning_activities.topic:
Topic 3: Audit Planning, Materiality and Risk

In Topic 3, we continue to learn how the audit risk model works. In particular, we will learn how acceptable audit risk and inherent risk are assessed. In addition, we explore how materiality thresholds are set at the planning stage and subsequently used to identify material misstatements. Relevant reading: Arens (Chapters 6 and 7)
learning_activities.topic_description: Topic 3: Audit Planning, Materiality and Risk In Topic 3, we continue to learn how the audit risk model works. In particular, we will learn how acceptable audit risk and inherent risk are assessed. In addition, we explore how materiality thresholds are set at the planning stage and subsequently used to identify material misstatements. Relevant reading: Arens (Chapters 6 and 7)

learning_activities.topic: Tutorial 3: Audit Planning, Materiality and Risk
learning_activities.topic_description: Tutorial 3: Audit Planning, Materiality and Risk

learning_activities.topic:
Topic 4: Internal Control, Control Risk and Fraud

In Topic 4, we will discuss the types of internal controls implemented by reporting entities that contribute to higher-quality financial information, and how control risk is assessed and tested by auditors. Relevant reading: Arens (Chapters 8, 9, and 10)
learning_activities.topic_title: Topic 4: Internal Control, Control Risk and Fraud
learning_activities.topic_description: Topic 4: Internal Control, Control Risk and Fraud In Topic 4, we will discuss the types of internal controls implemented by reporting entities that contribute to higher-quality financial information, and how control risk is assessed and tested by auditors. Relevant reading: Arens (Chapters 8, 9, and 10)

learning_activities.topic:
Topic 5: Tests of Controls and Substantive Tests 1

In Topic 5, we will introduce the concepts of tests of controls and substantive testing, and how these concepts, along with other concepts we have learned so far, are applied in the sales and collection cycle. Relevant reading: Arens (Chapters 11, 12, and 13).

Relevant reading: Arens (Chapters 11, 12, and 13).

Topic 6: Audit Sampling

In Topic 6, we will explore how sampling techniques are applied in audit testing, with practical examples. Relevant reading: Arens (Chapter 14).

learning_activities.topic:
Topic 7: Tests of Controls and Substantive Tests 2

In Topic 7, we will explore how tests of controls and substantive tests are applied in the audit of the following transaction cycles: acquisition and payment, inventory and warehousing, and payroll and personnel. Relevant reading: Arens (Chapter 15)

learning_activities.topic:
Topic 8: Tests of Controls and Substantive Tests 3

In Topic 8, we will explore how tests of controls and substantive tests are applied in the audit of balances of different accounts, including Cash and PPE accounts. Relevant Reading: Arens (Chapter 16)

learning_activities.topic:
Topic 9: Audit Reports

In Topic 9, we will demonstrate what the end product of the audit processes - an audit report - looks like, and the conditions under which each type of audit report is issued. Relevant reading: Arens (Chapter 18)

learning_activities.topic:
Topic 10: Completing the Audit

In topic 10, we will identify audit procedures to complete the audit process, including identifying and assessing subsequent events and contingent liabilities. Relevant Reading: Arens (Chapter 17).

learning_activities.topic:
Topic 11: Ethics, Auditor Legal liabilities

In Topic 11, we will explore the ethical requirements auditors need to follow. More importantly, we will examine the legal consequences auditors could face if they fail to provide quality services to their clients. Relevant reading: Arens (Chapters 2 and 3).
learning_activities.topic_description: Topic 11: Ethics, Auditor Legal liabilities In Topic 11, we will explore the ethical requirements auditors need to follow. More importantly, we will examine the legal consequences auditors could face if they fail to provide quality services to their clients. Relevant reading: Arens (Chapters 2 and 3).
